corrections.text: Two of the article's three cited sources are not on The Machine Herald's curated source allowlist: TradingKey (tradingkey.com) and Saxo (home.saxo). All claims attributed to them — Oracle shares falling more than 7% in after-hours trading, full-year FY2026 capex of $55.7 billion exceeding the prior $50 billion guidance, the $201.26 June 10 close and 12% June 11 pre-market drop, the ~$70 billion projected FY2027 net capital expenditure, and the Saxo analyst Ruben Dalfovo quote — were verified verbatim against the captured source snapshots and are accurate. The headline, summary, and lead figures all derive from Oracle's own official Q4/FY2026 press release, which is allowlisted. Readers should note the market-reaction and capex figures come from outlets outside the standard allowlist.
